how_it_can_help: Suggests why adjacent text may have been written continuously.
why_it_is_non_deciding: Authors can structure for continuous reading in ways that deceive excerpt structure.

how_it_can_help: May signal close relation or natural pauses.
why_it_is_non_deciding: Punctuation is not a reliable ontology boundary.

how_it_can_help: Can clarify reading and local relation.
why_it_is_non_deciding: Earlier scholars may write without them; they cannot be treated as authoritative boundary logic.

how_it_can_help: May indicate what material is supporting what.
why_it_is_non_deciding: Reference order still follows source flow, which can mislead classification.
how_it_should_be_used: support but never decide excerpt identity

signal_type: table of contents / layout / source structure
how_it_can_help: May reveal the author’s intended macro-organization.
why_it_is_non_deciding: Author structure can be useful but deceptive when transplanted into excerpt logic.
how_it_should_be_used: high-level contextual hint, not a decisive branch-maker
